CGAL::Isosurfacing::Cartesian_grid_3.name: CGAL::Isosurfacing::Cartesian_grid_3
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Cartesian_grid_3.brief: The class Cartesian_grid_3 represents a 3D Cartesian grid, that is the partition of an iso-cuboid into identical iso-cuboidal cells.
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Cartesian_grid_3.detailed: Is model of IsosurfacingPartition_3 The class Cartesian_grid_3 is one of the possible space partitioning data structures that can be used along with value and gradient fields to make up a domain. GeomTraits must be a model of IsosurfacingTraits_3 . MemoryPolicy whether the geometric locations of the grid vertices are stored or not. Possible values are CGAL::Isosurfacing::Cache_vertex_locations and CGAL::Isosurfacing::Do_not_cache_vertex_locations . CGAL::Isosurfacing::Marching_cubes_domain_3() CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_domain_3()

members.name: Cartesian_grid_3
members.brief: Default constructor.

members.name: Cartesian_grid_3
members.brief: creates a Cartesian grid with dimensions[0]*dimensions[1]*dimensions[2] grid vertices.
members.detailed: The grid covers the space described by the iso-cuboid span . span the geometric span of the grid dimensions the number of grid vertices in the x , y , and z directions gt the geometric traits all dimensions are strictly positive.
members.argsstring: (const Iso_cuboid_3 &span, const std::array< std::size_t, 3 > &dimensions, const Geom_traits &gt=Geom_traits())

members.name: Cartesian_grid_3
members.brief: creates a Cartesian grid with dimensions[0]*dimensions[1]*dimensions[2] grid vertices.
members.detailed: The grid covers the space described by the iso-cuboid span, itself described through two diagonal corners p and q . p the lexicographically smallest corner of the iso-cuboid q the lexicographically largest corner of the iso-cuboid dimensions the number of grid vertices in the x , y , and z directions gt the geometric traits p is lexicographically strictly smaller than q all dimensions are strictly positive.
members.argsstring: (const Point_3 &p, const Point_3 &q, const std::array< std::size_t, 3 > &dimensions, const Geom_traits &gt=Geom_traits())

members.name: Cartesian_grid_3
members.brief: creates a Cartesian grid using a prescribed grid step spacing .
members.detailed: The grid covers the space described by the iso-cuboid span . span the geometric span of the grid spacing the dimension of the paving cell, in the x , y , and z directions gt the geometric traits the diagonal of span has length a multiple of spacing

members.name: Cartesian_grid_3
members.brief: creates a Cartesian grid using a prescribed grid step.
members.detailed: The grid covers the space described by an iso-cuboid, itself described through two diagonal corners. p the lexicographically smallest corner of the iso-cuboid q the lexicographically largest corner of the iso-cuboid spacing the dimension of the paving cell, in the x , y , and z directions, respectively. gt the geometric traits p is lexicographically strictly smaller than q the diagonal of the iso-cuboid has length a multiple of spacing
members.argsstring: (const Point_3 &p, const Point_3 &q, const Vector_3 &spacing, const Geom_traits &gt=Geom_traits())

members.name: geom_traits
members.brief: returns the geometric traits class.

members.name: span
members.brief: returns an iso-cuboid representing the geometric span of the Cartesian grid.

members.name: xdim
members.brief: returns the number of grid vertices in the x direction.

members.name: ydim
members.brief: returns the number of grid vertices in the y direction.

members.name: zdim
members.brief: returns the number of grid vertices in the z direction.

members.name: spacing
members.brief: returns the spacing of the Cartesian grid, that is a vector whose coordinates are the grid steps in the x , y , and z directions, respectively.

members.name: linear_index
members.brief: returns the index of a grid cell given its indices (i.e., (k * y_dim + j) * x_dim + i ).
members.argsstring: (const std::size_t i, const std::size_t j, const std::size_t k) const

members.name: index
members.brief: returns the coordinates of the grid cell that contains a given point.
members.detailed: For points on the boundary between two cells, the smaller index is returned. p the point to be located p is inside the grid.

members.name: point
members.brief: returns the geometric location of the grid vertex described by its three indices.
members.detailed: Depending on the value of the template parameter MemoryPolicy , locations might not be stored but calculated on-the-fly. i the index in the x direction j the index in the y direction k the index in the z direction i < xdim() and j < ydim() and k < zdim() a const reference or a newly constructed point, depending on the caching policy.
members.argsstring: (const std::size_t i, const std::size_t j, const std::size_t k) const

CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dichotomy_edge_inter.name: CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dichotomy_edge_intersection
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dichotomy_edge_inter.brief: The class Dichotomy_edge_intersection uses a dichotomy to find the intersection point between an edge and the isosurface.
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dichotomy_edge_inter.detailed: Is model of IsosurfacingEdgeIntersectionOracle_3 This class is for example suitable to be used as the EdgeIntersectionOracle template parameter of isosurfacing domain classes when values are computed using an implicit function. It is not optimal to use this class when values are interpolated from discrete values since the intersection can be computed analytically in this case. CGAL::Isosurfacing::Linear_interpolation_edge_intersection CGAL::Isosurfacing::Marching_cubes_domain_3 CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_domain_3

members.name: Dichotomy_edge_intersection
members.brief: Constructor, enabling setting up the two criteria which can stop the dichotomy: either a threshold on the value (i.e., the difference between the isovalue and the value at the current point is smaller than relative_eps * isovalue ), or a maximum number of iterations.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: computes the intersection point between an edge and the isosurface.
members.detailed: The result (if it exists) is stored in p . Domain must be a model of IsosurfacingDomain_3 p_0 the location of the first vertex of the edge p_1 the location of the second vertex of the edge val_0 the value at the first vertex of the edge val_1 the value at the second vertex of the edge domain the isosurfacing domain isovalue the isovalue defining the isosurface with which we seek an intersection p the intersection point, if it exists true if the intersection point exists, false otherwise
members.argsstring: (const typename Domain::Geom_traits::Point_3 &p_0, const typename Domain::Geom_traits::Point_3 &p_1, const typename Domain::Geom_traits::FT val_0, const typename Domain::Geom_traits::FT val_1, const Domain &domain, const typename Domain::Geom_traits::FT isovalue, typename Domain::Geom_traits::Point_3 &p) const

CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_doma.name: CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_domain_3
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_doma.brief: A domain that can be used as input in the Dual Contouring algorithm.
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_doma.detailed: Is model of IsosurfacingDomainWithGradient_3 This class is essentially a wrapper around the different bricks provided by its template parameters: Partition provides the spatial partitioning, ValueField and GradientField the values and gradients that define the isosurface. The optional template parameter EdgeIntersectionOracle gives control over the method used to compute edge-isosurface intersection points. Partition must be a model of IsosurfacingPartition_3 ValueField must be a model of IsosurfacingValueField_3 GradientField must be a model of IsosurfacingGradientField_3 EdgeIntersectionOracle must be a model of IsosurfacingEdgeIntersectionOracle_3 CGAL::Isosurfacing::dual_contouring() CGAL::Isosurfacing::Marching_cubes_domain_3()

members.name: Dual_contouring_domain_3
members.brief: constructs a domain that can be used with the Dual Contouring algorithm.
members.detailed: partition the space partitioning data structure values a continuous field of scalar values, defined over the geometric span of partition gradients a continuous field of normalized vectors, defined over the geometric span of partition intersection_oracle the oracle for edge-isosurface intersection computation the domain class keeps a reference to the partition , values and gradients objects. As such, users must ensure that the lifetimes of these objects exceed that of the domain object.
members.argsstring: (const Partition &partition, const ValueField &values, const GradientField &gradients, const EdgeIntersectionOracle &intersection_oracle=EdgeIntersectionOracle())

CGAL::Isosurfacing::Finite_difference_gr.name: CGAL::Isosurfacing::Finite_difference_gradient_3
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Finite_difference_gr.brief: Class template for a gradient that is calculated using finite differences.
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Finite_difference_gr.detailed: Is model of IsosurfacingGradientField_3 This gradient function evaluates a value function at six points that are a given distance delta away from the queried point along the Cartesian axes. GeomTraits must be a model of IsosurfacingTraits_3 .

members.brief: creates a new instance of this gradient class.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns the value the gradient at a point in 3D space.
members.detailed: p the point at which the gradient is computed.

CGAL::Isosurfacing::Grid_vertex_memory_p.name: CGAL::Isosurfacing::Grid_vertex_memory_policy
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Grid_vertex_memory_p.brief: A policy to choose whether grid vertex locations should be cached, or recomputed at each access.
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Grid_vertex_memory_p.detailed: Tag a tag that is either Tag_true (locations are cached) or Tag_false (locations are not cached).

CGAL::Isosurfacing::partition_traits.name: CGAL::Isosurfacing::partition_traits
CGAL::Isosurfacing::partition_traits.brief: The class partition_traits is the API compatibility layer between a model of IsosurfacingPartition_3 and the isosurfacing domain classes CGAL::Isosurfacing::Marching_cubes_domain_3 and CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_domain_3 .
CGAL::Isosurfacing::partition_traits.detailed: For each model of IsosurfacingPartition_3 , a partial specialization of partition_traits must be provided, providing the types and functions listed below. Such a partial specialization is provided for CGAL::Isosurfacing::Cartesian_grid_3 .

members.name: vertex_descriptor
members.brief: A vertex descriptor corresponds to a unique vertex in an abstract partition instance.

members.name: edge_descriptor
members.brief: An edge descriptor corresponds to a unique edge in an abstract partition instance.

members.name: cell_descriptor
members.brief: A cell descriptor corresponds to a unique edge in an abstract partition instance.

members.name: Edge_vertices
members.brief: A container for the two vertices of an edge.
members.detailed: Must be a model of RandomAccessContainer whose value_type must be vertex_descriptor .

members.name: Cells_incident_to_edge
members.brief: A container for the cells incident to an edge.
members.detailed: Must be a model of ForwardRange whose value_type must be cell_descriptor .

members.name: Cell_vertices
members.brief: A container for the vertices of a cell.
members.detailed: Must be a model of ForwardRange whose value_type must be vertex_descriptor .

members.name: Cell_edges
members.brief: A container for the edges of a cell.
members.detailed: Must be a model of ForwardRange whose value_type must be edge_descriptor .

members.name: point
members.detailed: the 3D location of the vertex v .

members.name: incident_vertices
members.detailed: the two vertices incident to the edge e .

members.name: incident_cells
members.detailed: all the cells incident to the edge e , in a geometrically ordered manner around the edge.

members.name: cell_vertices
members.detailed: all the vertices of the cell c .

members.name: cell_edges
members.detailed: all the edges of the cell c .

members.name: for_each_vertex
members.brief: iterates over all vertices, and calls the functor f on each one.
members.detailed: ConcurrencyTag decides if the vertices are iterated sequentially or in parallel. Can be either CGAL::Sequential_tag , CGAL::Parallel_if_available_tag , or CGAL::Parallel_tag . Functor must implement void operator()(vertex_descriptor vertex) f the functor called on every vertex partition the partition whose vertices are being iterated over

members.name: for_each_edge
members.brief: iterates over all edges, and calls the functor f on each one.
members.detailed: ConcurrencyTag decides if the edges are iterated sequentially or in parallel. Can be either CGAL::Sequential_tag , CGAL::Parallel_if_available_tag , or CGAL::Parallel_tag . Functor must implement void operator()(edge_descriptor edge) . f the functor called on every edge partition the partition whose edges are being iterated over

members.name: for_each_cell
members.brief: iterates over all cells, and calls the functor f on each one.
members.detailed: ConcurrencyTag decides if the cells are iterated sequentially or in parallel. Can be either CGAL::Sequential_tag , CGAL::Parallel_if_available_tag , or CGAL::Parallel_tag . Functor must implement void operator()(cell_descriptor cell) . f the functor called on every cell partition the partition whose cells are being iterated over

CGAL::Isosurfacing::Trilinear_interpolat.name: CGAL::Isosurfacing::Trilinear_interpolation
CGAL::Isosurfacing::Trilinear_interpolat.detailed: Is model of IsosurfacingInterpolationScheme_3 The class Trilinear_interpolation is the standard interpolation scheme to define continuous fields of scalar values and gradients from data defined only at the vertices of a Cartesian grid. Grid must be CGAL::Isosurfacing::Cartesian_grid_3 <GeomTraits> , with GeomTraits a model of IsosurfacingTraits_3

members.argsstring: (const Point_3 &p, const Grid &g, const std::vector< FT > &values) const

members.argsstring: (const Point_3 &p, const Grid &g, const std::vector< Vector_3 > &gradients) const

IsosurfacingDomain_3.name: IsosurfacingDomain_3
IsosurfacingDomain_3.brief: The concept IsosurfacingDomain_3 describes the set of requirements to be fulfilled by any class used as input data for isosurfacing algorithms.
IsosurfacingDomain_3.detailed: A model of the concept IsosurfacingDomain_3 provides a partition of the Euclidean space in cells, and a scalar field defined over the whole partition. The isosurfacing algorithms traverse these cells and query the domain class at the vertices of each cell, using the functions point() and value() . Has models CGAL::Isosurfacing::Marching_cubes_domain_3 CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_domain_3 IsosurfacingDomainWithGradient_3

IsosurfacingDomainWithGradient_3.name: IsosurfacingDomainWithGradient_3
IsosurfacingDomainWithGradient_3.brief: The concept IsosurfacingDomainWithGradient_3 describes the set of requirements to be fulfilled by any class used as input data for some isosurfacing algorithms.
IsosurfacingDomainWithGradient_3.detailed: Refines IsosurfacingDomain_3 This concept refines IsosurfacingDomain_3 to add a gradient() function which is used by isosurfacing domains to query the domain for the gradient of the values field at a 3D query point (not necessarily a vertex) in space. members.brief: computes the intersection point between an edge and the isosurface.
members.detailed: Domain must be a model of IsosurfacingDomain_3 p_0 the location of the first vertex of the edge p_1 the location of the second vertex of the edge val_0 the value at the first vertex of the edge val_1 the value at the second vertex of the edge domain the isosurfacing domain isovalue the isovalue defining the isosurface with which we seek an intersection p the intersection point, if it exists true if the intersection point exists, false otherwise.
members.argsstring: (typename Domain::Geom_traits::Point_3 p_0, typename Domain::Geom_traits::Point_3 p_1, typename Domain::Geom_traits::FT val_0, typename Domain::Geom_traits::FT val_1, Domain domain, typename Domain::Geom_traits::FT isovalue, typename Domain::Geom_traits::Point_3 &p) const

IsosurfacingGradientField_3.name: IsosurfacingGradientField_3
IsosurfacingGradientField_3.brief: The concept IsosurfacingGradientField_3 describes the set of requirements to be fulfilled by the gradient field template parameter of the domain class CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_domain_3 .
IsosurfacingGradientField_3.detailed: Gradient fields must be continuous and defined over the geometric span of the space partitioning data structure (also known as "partition") being used. Has models CGAL::Isosurfacing::Gradient_function_3 CGAL::Isosurfacing::Finite_difference_gradient_3 CGAL::Isosurfacing::Interpolated_discrete_gradients_3 IsosurfacingTraits_3 IsosurfacingValueField_3

IsosurfacingPartition_3.name: IsosurfacingPartition_3
IsosurfacingPartition_3.brief: The concept IsosurfacingPartition_3 describes the set of requirements to be fulfilled by the partition template parameter of the domain classes CGAL::Isosurfacing::Marching_cubes_domain_3 and CGAL::Isosurfacing::Dual_contouring_domain_3 .
IsosurfacingPartition_3.detailed: A 3D partition is a space partitioning data structure that provides a discrete representation of a subset of 3D space. A partial specialization of CGAL::Isosurfacing::partition_traits must be provided for all models. This is similar to graph traits in PkgBGL. Has models CGAL::Isosurfacing::Cartesian_grid_3 CGAL::Octree

members.brief: The 3D cuboid type.

members.name: Compute_x_3
members.brief: A construction object that must provide the function operators:
members.detailed: FT operator()(Point_3 p) and FT operator()(Vector_3 p) which return the $ x$ -coordinate of the point and the vector, respectively.

members.name: Compute_y_3
members.brief: A construction object that must provide the function operators:
members.detailed: FT operator()(Point_3 p) and FT operator()(Vector_3 p) which return the $ y$ -coordinate of the point and the vector, respectively.

members.name: Compute_z_3
members.brief: A construction object that must provide the function operators:
members.detailed: FT operator()(Point_3 p) and FT operator()(Vector_3 p) which return the $ z$ -coordinate of the point and the vector, respectively.

members.name: Construct_point_3
members.brief: A construction object that must provide the function operator:
members.detailed: Point_3 operator()(FT x, FT y, FT z) which constructs a 3D point from its three coordinates.

members.name: Construct_vector_3
members.brief: A construction object that must provide the function operator:
members.detailed: Vector_3 operator()(FT x, FT y, FT z) which constructs a 3D vector from its three coordinates.

members.name: Construct_iso_cuboid_3
members.brief: A construction object that must provide the function operator:
members.detailed: IsoCuboid_3 operator()(Point_3 p, Point_3 q) which constructs an iso-oriented cuboid with diagonal opposite vertices p and q such that p is the lexicographically smallest point in the cuboid.

members.name: Construct_vertex_3
members.brief: A construction object that must provide the function operator:
members.detailed: Point_3 operator()(IsoCuboid_3 c, int i) which returns the i-th vertex of an iso-cuboid c . See Kernel::ConstructVertex_3 for the order of vertices.
